replies.msg:
應該用
assert isinstance(x, int), print("x must be an integer")

in_list = ['abcd','abcde','abc']


def long_string(in_list, str_len):
assert isinstance(str_len, int), print("str length must be an integer")
return [x for x in in_list if len(x) >= str_len]

long_string(in_list, 3)

def long_string(in_list, str_len):
        return [x for x in in_list if len(x) >= str_len] if isinstance(str_len, int) else "str length must be an integer"

如果想再短就要用埋 lambda

replies.msg:
唔應該係咁既
如果用 isinstance 就做到既野
唔應該用 try 同 except
係 running上佢係比較慢同容易出錯
更加唔應該try 晒成大段野

仲有
你個function input 唔應該用 list 做 variable name
應該list 本身係一個class
雖然你唔會出錯
但之後你就call唔番 list 呢個class出黎

先唔評論點解要用try except.
淨係try return果行唔會raise error.
而一般做error check都係第一行做
def long_string(input: list, x):
    assert isinstance(x, int), "x is not an integer"
    assert x > 0, "x must be larger than zero"
    ...

依個syntax掟出來個error叫AssertionError. 你用except TypeError都唔會catch到. (仲有list係keyword來, 唔好用來name)
